Title: Vendor Login — Valid Credentials
Preconditions: Vendor account exists (status: Active); Portal accessible; Browser: Chrome latest
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to vendor portal login page | 2. Enter email: vendor_test@testco.com | 3. Enter password: Test@1234! | 4. Click Login | 5. Observe redirect and session
Expected Result: Redirected to Vendor Dashboard; dashboard displays vendor name; session cookie set (HttpOnly, Secure); login event logged in audit log
Test Data: Email: vendor_test@testco.com | Password: Test@1234! | Expected redirect: /vendor/dashboard

Title: Account Lockout After N Failed Logins
Preconditions: Vendor account exists (status: Active); Lockout threshold configured at 5 attempts
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to login page | 2. Enter valid email; enter wrong password | 3. Repeat 5 times | 4. Observe error on 5th attempt | 5. Attempt login with correct password | 6. Observe response
Expected Result: After 5th fail: error "Account locked"; correct password refused while locked; DB status changes to Locked; alert email sent to vendor
Test Data: Email: vendor_test@testco.com | Wrong password: wrongpass123 (×5) | Correct password: Test@1234! | Lockout threshold: 5

Title: Session Expiry After Inactivity
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; session timeout = 30 minutes; vendor on dashboard
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in successfully | 2. Leave browser idle for 31 minutes | 3. Navigate to /vendor/invoices | 4. Observe response
Expected Result: Redirected to login; message "Session expired. Please log in again"; session token invalidated (API returns 401); no sensitive data via back button
Test Data: Session timeout: 30 min | Idle duration: 31 min | Navigation target: /vendor/invoices

Title: Invoice Submission — Happy Path
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; PO-2024-1001 exists (Open, ₹1,00,000); valid PDF prepared; invoice amount ₹75,000
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to Invoice Submission | 2. Click New Invoice | 3. Enter Invoice No: INV-TC-004-001 | 4. Enter PO: PO-2024-1001 | 5. Enter amount ₹75,000 (GST 18%) | 6. Upload valid_invoice_tc004.pdf (1.2 MB) | 7. Fill mandatory fields | 8. Click Submit
Expected Result: Success toast shown; invoice status = Submitted; DB record created with correct values; AP team notified; invoice in AP pending queue

Title: Duplicate Invoice Detection
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; Invoice INV-TC-004-001 already submitted (status: Submitted)
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to Invoice Submission | 2. Enter same Invoice No: INV-TC-004-001 | 3. Enter PO: PO-2024-1001 | 4. Fill remaining fields | 5. Click Submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked; error: "Invoice number INV-TC-004-001 already exists"; no duplicate DB record; invoice count unchanged; form retains data for correction
Test Data: Existing Invoice: INV-TC-004-001 (Submitted) | DB check: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM invoices WHERE invoice_number=INV-TC-004-001

Title: Invoice Amount Exceeds PO Value
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; PO-2024-1002 exists (Open, ₹50,000 remaining)
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to Invoice Submission | 2. Enter PO: PO-2024-1002 | 3. Confirm remaining value ₹50,000 displayed | 4. Enter amount: ₹60,000 | 5. Fill mandatory fields | 6. Click Submit
Expected Result: Submission blocked; error: "Invoice amount ₹60,000 exceeds remaining PO value of ₹50,000"; no DB record created; PO remaining value unchanged
Test Data: PO: PO-2024-1002 (Open, ₹50,000 remaining) | Invoice amount: ₹60,000

Title: AP Invoice Approval — Happy Path
Preconditions: AP Reviewer logged in; Invoice INV-TC-004-001 in Submitted status in AP queue; reviewer has APPROVE permission
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as ap_reviewer@company.com | 2. Navigate to AP Dashboard → Pending | 3. Open INV-TC-004-001 | 4. Verify invoice details | 5. Click Approve Invoice | 6. Confirm in dialog | 7. Observe status and notifications
Expected Result: Status changes to Approved; audit log entry created (user, action, timestamp); invoice moves to Approved queue; vendor receives approval email; invoice queued for payment processing
Test Data: AP User: ap_reviewer@company.com | Invoice: INV-TC-004-001 (Submitted, ₹75,000)

Title: AP Rejection with Mandatory Reason
Preconditions: AP Reviewer logged in; Invoice INV-TEST-008 in Submitted status
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to AP pending queue | 2. Open INV-TEST-008 | 3. Click Reject Invoice | 4. Attempt to submit with blank reason (observe validation) | 5. Enter reason: "Incorrect invoice amount" | 6. Click Confirm Rejection
Expected Result: Step 4: Cannot proceed — error "Rejection reason is required"; Step 6: Status changes to Rejected; audit log entry created; vendor receives rejection email with reason included
Test Data: AP User: ap_reviewer@company.com | Invoice: INV-TEST-008 | Reason tested blank then: "Incorrect invoice amount"

Title: Vendor Cannot Access AP Approval Screens
Preconditions: Vendor logged in as vendor_test@testco.com; AP URL known: /ap/invoices/pending
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as vendor_test@testco.com | 2. Type /ap/invoices/pending directly in browser | 3. Observe UI response | 4. Call GET /api/v1/ap/invoices/pending with vendor JWT via Postman
Expected Result: UI: 403 Forbidden or redirect to dashboard with "No permission" message; API: HTTP 403 with {"error":"Insufficient permissions"}; no AP data returned; unauthorized access attempt logged
Test Data: Vendor: vendor_test@testco.com | Target: /ap/invoices/pending | Vendor JWT used in API call

Title: File Upload Boundary Value Analysis
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; max file size = 5 MB; test files prepared at boundary sizes
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Upload 4.9 MB file | 2. Upload exactly 5.0 MB file | 3. Upload 5.1 MB file | 4. Observe result for each
Expected Result: 4.9 MB: Accepted; 5.0 MB: Accepted (confirm with BA); 5.1 MB: Rejected with error "File size exceeds 5 MB limit"; rejected file not stored
Test Data: File 1: 4.9 MB PDF | File 2: 5.0 MB PDF | File 3: 5.1 MB PDF | Max allowed: 5 MB

Title: POST /api/v1/invoices — API Contract Validation
Test Type: API Testing — Contract + Auth + Error Codes
Preconditions: Valid vendor JWT obtained; PO-2024-1001 exists (Open); Postman configured
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. POST valid payload → verify HTTP 201 | 2. Verify DB record | 3. POST missing invoice_number → verify HTTP 400 | 4. POST with expired JWT → verify HTTP 401 | 5. POST as AP role → verify HTTP 403 | 6. POST invoice_amount=0 → verify HTTP 400
Expected Result: HTTP 201 with invoice_id and status=SUBMITTED; DB record matches payload; 400 for missing fields; 401 for expired token; 403 for wrong role; 400 for zero amount
Test Data: Valid payload: {invoice_number:INV-API-011, po_number:PO-2024-1001, invoice_amount:75000} | Auth: Bearer <vendor_jwt>

Title: Payment Forwarding — Happy Path Integration
Preconditions: Invoice INV-TC-004-001 in Approved status; payment integration API endpoint configured on staging
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. AP Reviewer approves invoice (TC-007 precondition) | 2. Monitor integration logs | 3. Verify API call made to payment system | 4. Verify payload correctness | 5. Check invoice status update
Expected Result: POST request made to payment API with correct invoice data; HTTP 200 received from payment system; invoice status updates to Payment Initiated; integration log shows success entry
Test Data: Invoice: INV-TC-004-001 (Approved, ₹88,500) | Payment API: staging endpoint | Expected status: Payment Initiated

Title: Payment API Unavailable — Graceful Failure
Preconditions: Invoice approved; payment API endpoint set to simulate 503 Service Unavailable
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Approve an invoice | 2. Payment API returns 503 | 3. Observe system behavior | 4. Check invoice status | 5. Check admin notification | 6. Check for retry mechanism
Expected Result: Invoice status does NOT change to failed silently; status shows "Payment Pending / Queued"; admin/AP team receives alert email; retry scheduled; invoice not lost from queue
Test Data: Invoice: test invoice (Approved) | Payment API: configured to return 503 | Expected: graceful queue + alert

Title: Vendor Receives Email on Invoice Submission
Preconditions: Vendor email configured; SMTP service active on staging; invoice submitted successfully
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Submit a valid invoice (as per TC-004) | 2. Wait up to 2 minutes | 3. Check vendor email inbox | 4. Verify email content
Expected Result: Email received within 2 minutes; subject includes invoice number; body contains PO number, amount, submission date; link to portal included; from address matches configured sender
Test Data: Vendor email: vendor_test@testco.com | Invoice: INV-TC-014-001 | Expected delivery: ≤2 minutes

Title: Monthly Report Data Accuracy
Preconditions: Multiple invoices in various statuses for November 2024; AP Manager logged in
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as AP Manager | 2. Navigate to Reports → Monthly Invoice Report | 3. Select period: November 2024 | 4. Generate report | 5. Compare report counts with direct DB query | 6. Verify amounts match
Expected Result: Report generates without error; submitted/approved/rejected/paid counts match DB query results; total value accurate to 2 decimal places; vendor breakdown present
Test Data: Test period: November 2024 | DB validation: SELECT status, COUNT(*), SUM(amount) FROM invoices WHERE created_month=2024-11 GROUP BY status

Title: AP User Cannot Submit Invoice on Behalf of Vendor
Preconditions: AP Reviewer logged in as ap_reviewer@company.com
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as ap_reviewer@company.com | 2. Navigate directly to /vendor/invoices/new | 3. Attempt via API: POST /api/v1/invoices with AP JWT | 4. Observe response
Expected Result: UI: 403 Forbidden or redirect; API: HTTP 403 Forbidden; no invoice record created in DB; attempt logged in security audit log
Test Data: AP User: ap_reviewer@company.com | Target: /vendor/invoices/new | API: POST /api/v1/invoices with AP JWT

Title: Vendor Registration — Happy Path
Preconditions: Portal accessible; email not already registered; SMTP active
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to registration page | 2. Enter company name: TestCo Pvt Ltd | 3. Enter email: new_vendor@testco.com | 4. Enter GSTIN: 27AABCT1332L1ZV | 5. Enter PAN: AABCT1332L | 6. Enter password: Test@1234! | 7. Click Register | 8. Check inbox
Expected Result: Account created with status=Pending Verification; confirmation email received within 2 minutes; vendor cannot log in until AP Admin approves; registration event in audit log
Test Data: Email: new_vendor@testco.com | Password: Test@1234! | GSTIN: 27AABCT1332L1ZV | PAN: AABCT1332L

Title: Vendor Registration — Validation Failures
Preconditions: Portal accessible
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Submit with blank email | 2. Submit with invalid email: notanemail | 3. Submit with weak password: abc123 | 4. Submit with invalid GSTIN: 12345 | 5. Observe error for each
Expected Result: Blank email: 'Email is required'; Invalid email: 'Enter a valid email address'; Weak password: 'Password must be 8+ chars with uppercase, number, and special character'; Invalid GSTIN: 'Enter a valid 15-character GSTIN'; no DB record in any case
Test Data: Invalid email: notanemail | Weak password: abc123 | Invalid GSTIN: 12345

Title: Invoice Submission — Invalid or Expired PO
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; PO-2023-EXPIRED status=Closed in DB; PO-9999-GHOST does not exist
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Enter PO: PO-9999-GHOST and attempt submit | 2. Enter PO: PO-2023-EXPIRED and attempt submit | 3. Observe error for each
Expected Result: PO-9999-GHOST: 'PO not found'; PO-2023-EXPIRED: 'PO is closed and cannot accept new invoices'; submission blocked in both cases; no DB record created
Test Data: Non-existent PO: PO-9999-GHOST | Closed PO: PO-2023-EXPIRED

Title: Invoice Submission — Missing Mandatory Fields
Preconditions: Vendor logged in
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Leave Invoice Number blank; fill all other fields; click Submit | 2. Leave PO Number blank; fill all other fields; click Submit | 3. Leave file upload empty; fill all other fields; click Submit
Expected Result: Each attempt blocked with field-specific error: 'Invoice Number is required' / 'PO Number is required' / 'Invoice file is required'; no DB record created; form retains entered data
Test Data: All mandatory fields tested individually as blank

Title: Invoice Edit Before Submission
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; invoice form partially filled but not submitted
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Fill all fields including amount: ₹50
Expected Result: 000 | 2. Do NOT click Submit | 3. Change amount to ₹60
Test Data: 000 | 4. Change invoice date | 5. Click Submit
Defect ID: 000 and revised date) reflected in submission confirmation and DB; original draft values not stored

Title: Concurrent AP Access — Race Condition Prevention
Preconditions: Two AP reviewer accounts active; Invoice INV-RACE-001 in Submitted status
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. AP Reviewer 1 opens INV-RACE-001 | 2. AP Reviewer 2 opens same invoice in separate session | 3. Reviewer 1 clicks Approve | 4. Reviewer 2 clicks Reject at same time | 5. Check final status and audit log
Expected Result: Only one action succeeds; second reviewer sees error: 'This invoice is currently being actioned by another reviewer'; final status reflects one decision only; audit log records both attempts
Test Data: Two concurrent sessions on invoice: INV-RACE-001

Title: AP Audit Trail — All Actions Logged
Preconditions: AP Reviewer logged in; DB/audit log accessible
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. AP Reviewer approves INV-AUDIT-001 | 2. Query audit_log: SELECT * WHERE invoice_id='INV-AUDIT-001' | 3. Verify log entry fields | 4. Attempt DELETE on audit record | 5. Repeat with rejection on INV-AUDIT-002
Expected Result: Each action has immutable audit entry with: action_type; user_id; timestamp (UTC); invoice_id; reason (for rejection); DELETE on audit table returns 403/permission denied
Test Data: Invoices: INV-AUDIT-001 (approve) | INV-AUDIT-002 (reject)

Title: Vendor Receives Email on Invoice Approval
Preconditions: AP Reviewer approves invoice; SMTP active on staging
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. AP Reviewer approves INV-TC-024-001 | 2. Wait up to 2 minutes | 3. Check vendor inbox | 4. Verify subject and body
Expected Result: Email received within 2 minutes; subject: 'Invoice [INV-TC-024-001] Approved'; body contains invoice number, approved amount, and portal link
Test Data: Vendor: vendor_test@testco.com | Invoice: INV-TC-024-001

Title: Vendor Receives Rejection Email Including Reason
Preconditions: AP Reviewer rejects invoice with reason; SMTP active
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. AP Reviewer rejects INV-TC-025-001 with reason: 'GST amount incorrect' | 2. Wait up to 2 minutes | 3. Check vendor inbox | 4. Verify reason present in email body
Expected Result: Email received within 2 minutes; reason 'GST amount incorrect' present in body (not blank); invoice number and AP contact included
Test Data: Invoice: INV-TC-025-001 | Rejection reason: GST amount incorrect

Title: AP Team Receives Alert on New Invoice Submission
Preconditions: Vendor submits invoice; AP team group email configured; SMTP active
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Vendor submits valid invoice INV-TC-026-001 | 2. Wait up to 2 minutes | 3. Check ap_team@company.com inbox | 4. Verify content
Expected Result: AP team email received within 2 minutes; subject: 'New Invoice Pending Review'; invoice number, vendor name, and amount present; link to AP dashboard included
Test Data: AP team email: ap_team@company.com | Invoice: INV-TC-026-001

Title: Monthly Report — Vendor Sees Own Data Only
Preconditions: Vendor A has invoices INV-A-001; Vendor B has invoices INV-B-001; AP Manager account exists
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as vendor_a@testco.com | 2. View invoice history | 3. Verify INV-A-001 visible | 4. Verify INV-B-001 NOT visible | 5. Log in as AP Manager | 6. Generate full monthly report — verify both vendors present
Expected Result: Vendor A sees only own invoices; INV-B-001 inaccessible; cross-vendor data isolation confirmed; AP Manager sees all vendors in report
Test Data: Vendor A: vendor_a@testco.com (INV-A-001) | Vendor B: vendor_b@otherco.com (INV-B-001)

Title: Unauthenticated Direct URL Access Redirects to Login
Preconditions: No active session; browser cookies cleared
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Open browser with no session | 2. Navigate to /vendor/dashboard | 3. Navigate to /vendor/invoices | 4. Navigate to /ap/invoices/pending | 5. Observe each
Expected Result: All three URLs redirect to login page; no portal content rendered without session; intended URL preserved as return-URL so user lands on correct page after login
Test Data: Target URLs: /vendor/dashboard | /vendor/invoices | /ap/invoices/pending

Title: Invoice Upload — Invalid File Format Rejected
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; files prepared in XLSX and JPG formats
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Upload invoice.xlsx | 2. Observe error | 3. Upload invoice.jpg | 4. Observe error | 5. Upload invoice.pdf | 6. Observe result
Expected Result: XLSX: 'Only PDF files are accepted'; JPG: 'Only PDF files are accepted'; PDF accepted and uploaded successfully; rejected files not stored on server
Test Data: Test files: invoice.xlsx | invoice.jpg | invoice.pdf

Title: Cumulative PO Billing — Third Invoice Exceeds Ceiling
Preconditions: PO-2024-1003 has ceiling ₹1,00,000; INV-A (₹60,000) and INV-B (₹30,000) already approved against it; ₹10,000 remaining
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as vendor | 2. Submit new invoice against PO-2024-1003 for ₹20
Expected Result: 000 | 3. Observe validation
Test Data: Submission blocked; error: 'Invoice amount ₹20,000 exceeds remaining PO value of ₹10,000'; system shows cumulative billed amount and remaining balance; no DB record created
Execution Status: PO-2024-1003: ceiling ₹1,00,000 | Previously invoiced: ₹90,000 | New invoice: ₹20,000 | Remaining: ₹10,000

Title: Password Reset — Self-Service Flow
Preconditions: Vendor account active; SMTP active; vendor has access to registered email
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to login page | 2. Click 'Forgot Password' | 3. Enter vendor_test@testco.com | 4. Check inbox | 5. Click reset link in email | 6. Enter new password: NewPass@5678! | 7. Log in with new password
Expected Result: Reset email received within 2 minutes; link expires after 15 minutes; new password accepted; old password no longer works; password change logged in audit trail
Test Data: Email: vendor_test@testco.com | New password: NewPass@5678!

Title: Password Reset Link — Expiry After 15 Minutes
Preconditions: Password reset email received and link noted; 16 minutes elapsed
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Request password reset for vendor_test@testco.com | 2. Wait 16 minutes | 3. Click reset link | 4. Attempt to set new password
Expected Result: Page shows 'This password reset link has expired. Please request a new one.'; no password change occurs; vendor must request a fresh link
Test Data: Email: vendor_test@testco.com | Wait: 16 minutes

Title: Suspended Vendor Cannot Log In
Preconditions: Vendor account status = Suspended by AP Admin; vendor attempts login
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as suspended_vendor@testco.com with correct password | 2. Observe response | 3. Verify vendor cannot access any portal page
Expected Result: Login rejected; error: 'Your account has been suspended. Please contact support.'; no session created; no redirect to dashboard
Test Data: Vendor: suspended_vendor@testco.com | Account status: Suspended

Title: Invoice Withdrawal Before AP Review
Preconditions: Vendor logged in; INV-WITHDRAW-001 in Submitted status; AP review not yet started
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Navigate to My Invoices | 2. Open INV-WITHDRAW-001 | 3. Click Withdraw Invoice | 4. Confirm withdrawal | 5. Check invoice status | 6. Check AP team inbox
Expected Result: Invoice status changes to Cancelled; AP team receives notification: 'Invoice [INV-WITHDRAW-001] has been withdrawn by vendor'; invoice removed from AP pending queue; vendor can no longer edit it
Test Data: Invoice: INV-WITHDRAW-001 | Status before: Submitted

Title: High-Value Invoice Requires AP Manager Approval
Preconditions: Invoice INV-HIGH-001 amount: ₹6,00,000; AP Reviewer logged in (can only approve ≤₹5,00,000)
Steps (pipe-separated): 1. Log in as ap_reviewer@company.com | 2. Open INV-HIGH-001 in pending queue | 3. Attempt to click Approve | 4. Log in as ap_manager@company.com | 5. Open same invoice | 6. Approve it
Expected Result: Step 3: Approve button disabled or error: 'Invoices above ₹5,00,000 require AP Manager approval'; Step 6: Approval succeeds; status changes to Approved
Test Data: Invoice: INV-HIGH-001 | Amount: ₹6,00,000 | AP Reviewer threshold: ₹5,00,000
